title: salix alba var. tristis
intro: Salix alba var. tristis, also known as white willow bark, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its dark green leaves and white, papery bark. The medicinal properties of white willow bark lie in its high concentration of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The health benefits of white willow bark include reducing inflammation and pain associated with arthritis, menstrual cramps, and headaches, as well as its potential to lower fever. White willow bark is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and can be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
